title: Mapping Torus of the Antipodal Map on $S^1$

solution.answer: $T_f$ is homeomorphic to the Klein bottle $K$. So $H_0 \cong \mathbb{Z}$, $H_1 \cong \mathbb{Z} \oplus \mathbb{Z}/2$, $H_n = 0$ for $n \geq 2$.
steps: $T_f = S^1 \times [0,1]/(e^{i\theta},1) \sim (e^{-i\theta}, 0)$. The cylinder is glued at its two boundary circles, but with a reflection, producing a non-orientable surface.

In terms of the square $[0,1]^2$ model: identify top and bottom edges in the same direction (forming a cylinder) and then identify left and right edges in opposite directions. This is precisely the Klein bottle.
Formally: in the polygonal model, the attaching word becomes $aba^{-1}b^{-1}$... actually $abab^{-1}$ for the Klein bottle depending on convention. Either way, $T_f \cong K$.
The free generator of $H_1$ corresponds to the base circle of the fibration, the $\mathbb{Z}/2$ generator corresponds to a fibre circle traversed twice.
